sections.heading: Create an Organization
sections.body:
When you create an organization, you are added as an
[Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) for the organization.

- Expand the Organizations menu in the navigation bar.

- Click View All Organizations.

> **Important:** Don't include [sensitive information](sensitive-info) in
> your organization name.

You have the option of adding a new Cloud Manager organization or a new
Atlas organization. For more information on Cloud Manager see the
[documentation](cloud-manager).

- For existing Atlas users, enter their username. Usually, this is
  the email the person used to register.

- For new Atlas users, enter their email address to send an invitation.

When you create a new organization with the Atlas UI, Atlas
requires IP access lists for the Atlas Administration API by default. If
you require an IP access list, your Atlas Administration API keys can
make API (Application Programming Interface) requests only from the location-based IP or CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing)
addresses that you specify in the IP access list.

To disable the IP access list requirement and allow your
Atlas Administration API keys to make requests from any address on the internet, toggle
Require IP Access List for the Atlas Administration API
to OFF.

To learn more, see [Optional: Require an IP Access List](atlas-require-api-access-list).

sections.heading: View Organizations
sections.body:
**Atlascli:**

Atlas CLI

To list all organizations using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas organizations list [options]

```

To return the details for the organization you specify using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas organizations describe <ID> [options]

```

To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas organizations list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-organizations-list) and
[atlas organizations describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-organizations-describe).

Related Links

- [Install the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/install-atlas-cli)

- [Connect to the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/connect-atlas-cli)

**Ui:**

Atlas UI

sections.heading: Leave an Organization
sections.body:
> **Important:** To leave an organization, at least another user must exist as an
> Owner for the organization.

- Expand the Organizations menu in the navigation bar.

- Click View All Organizations.

For the organization you wish to leave, click its Leave
button to bring up the Leave Organization dialog box.

sections.heading: Rename an Organization
sections.body:
You must have the [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) role for an
organization to rename it.

sections.heading: Delete an Organization
sections.body:
> **Important:** To delete an organization, you must have [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) role for the organization.
>
> You can't delete an organization that has active projects. You must
> delete the organization's projects before you can delete the
> organization.
>
> You can't delete an organization with outstanding payments. To
> learn more, see [Troubleshoot Invoices and Payments](troubleshoot-invoice-payment).
>
> If you have a [Backup Compliance Policy enabled](backup-compliance-policy), you
> can't [Delete a Project](delete-project) if any snapshots
> exists. If you can't remove all projects, you can't
> [delete the organization](delete-organization).

**Atlascli:**

Atlas CLI

To delete an organization using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas organizations delete <ID> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas organizations delete](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-organizations-delete).

**Ui:**

Atlas UI

In the General Settings tab, click Delete.
This displays the Delete Organization dialog box.
